Instructions for integrating MongoDB with Flask using MongoEngine and Flask-MongoEngine.

# Purpose
This document provides guidance on integrating MongoDB with a Python application using the `MongoEngine` library, which acts as a document mapper. It outlines the necessary setup, including the installation of `Flask-MongoEngine` and configuration of `MONGODB_SETTINGS` within a Flask application. The document explains how to define models that represent MongoDB documents by creating classes that inherit from `Document` and specifying fields. It also covers the creation of nested fields using `EmbeddedDocument` and `EmbeddedDocumentField`. Instructions for creating and saving data instances are provided, along with methods for querying the database using the `objects` attribute and query operators. The document concludes by directing users to additional resources for more advanced usage of `MongoEngine` and `Flask-MongoEngine`.
# Content Summary
This document provides instructions for integrating MongoDB with a Python application using the MongoEngine library, which acts as a document mapper. It outlines the necessary setup and configuration steps, including the requirement for a running MongoDB server and the installation of the `Flask-MongoEngine` package via pip.

The configuration section explains how to set up the MongoDB connection by defining `MONGODB_SETTINGS` in the Flask application's configuration and creating a `MongoEngine` instance. This setup connects the application to a MongoDB database named "myapp."

The document describes how to map MongoDB documents to Python classes by creating classes that inherit from `Document`. Each field in the MongoDB document is represented by a field in the class, such as `StringField` or `IntField`. For documents with nested fields, the document uses `EmbeddedDocument` and `EmbeddedDocumentField` to define and include embedded documents.

To create data, instantiate the document class with field values and call the `save()` method to store the document in the database. The document also explains how to perform queries using the `objects` attribute of the class. Queries can be refined using keyword arguments and query operators, which are appended to field names with a double-underscore.

The document concludes by directing users to the official MongoEngine and Flask-MongoEngine documentation for more advanced features and utilities.
